mission.title: Install and Configure VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid
mission.description: VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid is a distribution that provides a comprehensive platform for managing Kubernetes clusters in an enterprise environment. It leverages the Tanzu Framework and Carvel packaging to enhance the user experience and streamline operations. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management versus the robust features and integrations it offers.

steps.title: Install the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Tanzu CLI, which is essential for managing Tanzu Kubernetes Grid.

```bash

curl -L https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/tanzu-framework/releases/download/v0.29.0/tanzu-framework-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/tanzu

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/tanzu

```

> Download the Tanzu CLI binary.
> Make the Tanzu CLI executable.

steps.title: Initialize the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step initializes the Tanzu CLI and sets up the necessary configurations.

```bash

tanzu init

```

> Initialize the Tanzu CLI environment.

steps.title: Create a Tanzu Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Tanzu Kubernetes cluster with specified parameters.

```bash

tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --region us-west1 --node-count 3

```

> Create a cluster named 'my-cluster' with 3 nodes.
commands: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --region us-west1 --node-count 3

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to access the newly created cluster.

```bash

tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context

```

> Get the kubeconfig for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step includes optional configurations for autoscaling and monitoring.

```bash

tanzu package install metrics-server --package-name metrics-server.tanzu.vmware.com --version 0.4.4 --namespace kube-system

tanzu cluster autoscaler enable my-cluster

```

> Install metrics server for monitoring.
> Enable autoscaling for the cluster.
commands: tanzu package install metrics-server --package-name metrics-server.tanzu.vmware.com --version 0.4.4 --namespace kube-system

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Tanzu Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Tanzu Kubernetes cluster and may result in data loss. Ensure to backup any important data before proceeding.

```bash

tanzu cluster delete my-cluster

kubectl delete namespace my-cluster

```

> Delete the cluster named 'my-cluster'.
> Remove the namespace associated with the cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Tanzu Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to backup your cluster data. This step upgrades the cluster to a new version.

```bash

tanzu cluster backup my-cluster --namespace kube-system

tanzu cluster upgrade my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.29.1

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup the cluster before upgrading.
> Upgrade the cluster to Kubernetes version 1.29.1.
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: API server not reachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server is not accessible, possibly due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your network settings and ensure that the API server is reachable.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** One or more nodes are not in a 'Ready' state.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get nodes' to check the status and troubleshoot any failing nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to create cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified node count exceeds available resources.

**Fix:**
Ensure your environment meets the minimum resource requirements and adjust the node count.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Metrics server not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The metrics server is not installed or not running.

**Fix:**
Install the metrics server using 'tanzu package install metrics-server'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.29, improved CLI commands for cluster management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated older Kubernetes versions (1.25 and below).
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.4, ensure to backup your clusters and follow the upgrade commands.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' state, confirming the cluster is operational.
codeSnippets: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --region us-west1 --node-count 3
tanzu package install metrics-server --package-name metrics-server.tanzu.vmware.com --version 0.4.4 --namespace kube-system

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
